person_name: Gildo, Comes Africae (d. 398), rebel against Honorius
person_name_full: Gildo, Comes Africae (d. 398), rebel against Honorius
notes: Gildo, Comes Africae, a rebellious Roman military commander in North Africa who rebelled against Emperor Honorius and cut off Rome's grain supply.

docs.title: Letter 4003
docs.author: Quintus Aurelius Symmachus
docs.subject_summary:
Qoid de Afrorum dolore et militarium virorum qaerellis consaltus praecepto sacro 
amplissimas ordo censuerit, plene atque aperte gestorum carialium inspectione cog- 
nosces. sed quia me quoque faroili
docs.excerpt: … detailing Gildo's crimes [Gildo was a rebellious military commander in North Africa who cut off Rome's grain supply], an equal wave of outrage swept through the good men present.
